source.definition: to touch someone or something with the lips as a sign of affection or greeting

explanation.sourceText: The English word "kiss" comes from Old English "cyssan", which means to touch with the lips as a sign of love or affection.
explanation.targetText: Cuvântul englezesc „kiss” provine din engleza veche „cyssan”, care înseamnă a atinge cu buzele ca semn de dragoste sau afecțiune.

source.additional: Regular verb. Commonly used to express affection or as a friendly greeting.

target.definition: a face o atingere cu buzele unei persoane sau unui obiect

explanation.sourceText: The Romanian verb "a săruta" is an internal derivation from the noun "sărut" (kiss), whose origin is uncertain.
explanation.targetText: Verbul românesc „a săruta” este format intern din substantivul „sărut”, de origine incertă.

target.additional: Regular verb. Frequently used to express affection between lovers or friends.

learningTips.text: Remember the English word "kiss" by thinking of the soft sound you make when you press your lips together.
Associate "a săruta" with the English word "syrup" to recall the sweetness of a kiss.
Think of the phrase "kiss and tell" to remember the verb "a săruta" as an action involving lips and affection.
Imagine giving a kiss as a gentle 'sigh' sound, similar to the Romanian pronunciation /a səruˈta/.
